sourceMovie.title: The Circle
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto The Circle (2017) as a drama theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The drama and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: WarGames
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Circle clicked for you, WarGames (1983) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and thriller, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: The Usual Suspects
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Circle clicked for you, The Usual Suspects (1995) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Circle clicked for you, Minority Report (2002) is a strong next stop: both trade in thriller and science fiction, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Circle?
faq.answer: Try WarGames, The Usual Suspects, Minority Report—each shares DNA with The Circle while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Circle?
faq.answer: The Circle (2017) is best described as Drama, Thriller, Science Fiction. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Circle worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 5.5/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
